prs.title: [CI][AMD][BugFix] Patch has_flashinfer decorator for test_select_rocm_aiter_backend
prs.author: rasmith
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
The `has_flashinfer` function has been moved from `vllm.model_executor.layers.fused_moe.oracle.unquantized` to 
` vllm.utils.flashinfer.has_flashinfer ` but the patch decorator [here ](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/tests/kernels/moe/test_unquantized_backend_selection.py#L88) hasn't been updated.  This PR updates the patch decorator so the test now passes.

prs.title: [ROCm][Bugfix]: W4A4 MOE using emulation instead of AITER on MXFP4-supported hardware
prs.author: Rohan138
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
#39801 accidentally switched off `rocm_aiter_fused_moe` for all quark-quantized W4A4 models, forcing them to use the `MXFP4.Emulation` backend instead. The correct list should be W4A16, W4A8 and W4A4, see: https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/blob/main/csrc/ck_gemm_moe_2stages_codegen/gen_instances.py#L767
https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/blob/main/csrc/ck_tile_gemm_moe_2stages/moe_cktile2stages_common.py#L252

prs.title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Fix gemm_a4w4 call to use updated AITER API signature
prs.author: chelnnexy
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
 Fix `gemm_a4w4` call in `quark_ocp_mx.py` to use the updated AITER API signature.

  AITER changed `gemm_a4w4` to remove the preallocated output tensor parameter and return the output instead.

  ```python 
 Old call:
  gemm_a4w4(A, B, A_scale, B_scale, out, bpreshuffle=True)

  New API:
  y = gemm_a4w4(A, B, A_scale, B_scale, dtype=out_dtype, bpreshuffle=True)
 ```
  Without this fix, the output tensor is silently passed as the bias argument, breaking MXFP4 GEMM on ROCm for Quark OCP MX
   quantized models.
 

## Test Plan
  Run vLLM with an MXFP4-quantized model on ROCm with AITER installed:
  ```
  VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_FP4_ASM_GEMM=1 vllm serve <mxfp4-model>
  ```
Run accuracy validation on Llama-3.1-405B-MXFP4 after applying the fix.

## Test Result

  **Before:**

> Output tensor was passed as bias
> Incorrect MXFP4 GEMM behavior on ROCm
> Accuracy check failed

  **After:** 

> Call site uses the new AITER return-value API correctly
> Model loads and runs correctly with MXFP4 GEMM on ROCm
> Validated on MI355X with Llama-3.1-405B-MXFP4
> Accuracy check passes

---

prs.title: [Frontend][RFC] Rust front-end integration
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
See corresponding RFC for introducing a rust-based alternative front-end process in vLLM https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/40846.

For now we have staged the poc implementation in https://github.com/Inferact/vllm-frontend-rs. This PR contains the logic to integrate it into vLLM.

You can try it out by building this branch:

```shell
# Full install
uv pip install .

# Or use pre-compiled wheel
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 uv pip install --editable .
./build_rust.sh

# Run it
VLLM_USE_RUST_FRONTEND=1 vllm serve ...
```

Co-authored with @BugenZhao

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Optimize ROCm Docker build: registry cache, DeepEP, and ci-bake script
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Implements the three-tier Docker build for ROCm CI. Every PR currently rebuilds RIXL, DeepEP, rocshmem, torchcodec, and RDMA libraries from scratch, costing a total of 26 minutes on average per build. This PR introduces a pre-built Tier-1 `ci_base` image that absorbs those stable layers. Per-PR builds then only rebuild the thin vLLM wheel + workspace layer.

**Image registry layout after this PR:**

| Tag | Built by | Frequency |
|-----|----------|-----------|
| `rocm/vllm-dev:base` | `Dockerfile.rocm_base` | Monthly |
| `rocm/vllm-dev:ci_base` | `ci_base` stage (this PR) | Weekly |
| `rocm/vllm-ci:$COMMIT` | `test` stage (this PR) | Per PR |

---

This PR is connected to: https://github.com/vllm-project/ci-infra/pull/307
These two PRs should likely be merged simultaneously.

cc @kenroche @okakarpa @tjtanaa @gshtras @khluu

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Add K8s-hardened minimal Python CI runner with JUnit exit-code fix and resource watchdog
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Replaces the 500-line bash `run-amd-test.sh` with Python script (`run-amd-test.py`) that is easier to read, debug, and extend. The bash script becomes a thin shim that delegates to the Python implementation.

The new script handles:
- `amd-smi` GPU integrity verification
- Docker pull with retry (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
- Container execution with configurable timeout; kills and returns exit code 124 on timeout
- Health watchdog with background thread samples memory, host memory, disk usage, GPU VRAM, and container state every 60s. It also writes pre/post snapshots and all samples to `health_watchdog.log`
- JUnit exit-code override that promotes exit 0 to 1 when `results.xml` reports failures (catches `os._exit(0)` masking real failures)

cc @kenroche @okakarpa @dhonnappa-amd @tjtanaa

prs.title: [vLLM IR] 2/N fused_add_rms_norm and maybe_inplace overload
prs.author: ProExpertProg
prs.body_head:
# [vLLM IR] 2/N Add maybe_inplace overload for in-place operation support

## Summary
This PR extends the vLLM IR framework to support optional in-place semantics 
(reusing input activation memory for outputs) via a new `maybe_inplace` overload.
It includes dispatching/registration infrastructure and additional compiler passes
to manage functionalization through the compilation pipeline.
Finally, this PR ports the `fused_add_rms_norm` to IR using the `maybe_inplace` overload.

Key features:
- **`maybe_inplace` overload**: Ops can allow in-place implementations to reuse activation tensor memory
- **eager-mode functionalization**: When the default overload dispatches to an inplace implementation,
activations are cloned to preserve their contents
- **E2E Compilation pipeline integration**: New and existing passes manage IR ops through the compilation pipeline, 
from functionalization to lowering and clone elimination.
- **Fusion passes updated**: Now using the `fused_add_rms_norm` IR op instead of 
- **IR documentation**: Added a full doc on all vLLM IR features: registration, dispatching, compilation, and overall design.
- **Unit & integration tests**: IR infra, kernels, compilation, etc.

## Motivation

vLLM IR separates operation **semantics** from **implementation**, enabling multiple implementations per op.
However, some kernels might optimize memory usage by reusing input activation buffers for outputs.
This poses two problems: (1) torch.compile does not like in-place operations, 
and (2) not all implementations of the IR op will have the same behavior.

To solve this, we introduce the `maybe_inplace` overload, which is allowed (but not required)
to alias output tensors to inputs. When a user calls the `maybe_inplace` overload, it signals
to the dispatcher & compiler that the inputs are no longer needed and their memory may be reused for outputs.

## Changes

### Core IR (`vllm/ir/op.py`)

1. **Extended `@register_op` decorator**

prs.title: [vLLM IR][Rope] Port RotaryEmbedding and DeepseekScalingRotaryEmbedding to IR Ops
prs.author: wxsIcey
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Port `RotaryEmbedding` and `DeepseekScalingRotaryEmbedding` to vLLM IR, following https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/38756. For the base `RotaryEmbedding`, keep the `key=None` path out of the IR `maybe_inplace` call and fall back to the existing static implementation.

This preserves cross-layer KV sharing behavior and avoids `torch.library` schema inference failures on optional returns.


## Test Plan
To be tested

## Test Result

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Add missing quantization methods and fix online quant test failures
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Several quantization methods were failing on ROCm because they weren't registered in the platform's `supported_quantization` list, even though the underlying kernels already support them (either natively or through emulation fallbacks).

This adds `fp8_per_tensor`, `fp8_per_block`, `online`, `gpt_oss_mxfp4`, `mxfp8`, `modelopt`, `modelopt_mxfp8`, and `modelopt_mixed` to ROCm's supported quantization allowlist. These all have working codepaths on ROCm. FP8 variants use `ROCmFP8ScaledMMLinearKernel`, mxfp8 and modelopt_mxfp8 use `EmulationMxfp8LinearKernel` (introduced in #39205), and the online quant schemes resolve to fp8 methods that already work.

Also fixes two issues in the test infrastructure that were blocking the online quantization peak memory test on ROCm:

1. The peak GPU memory log in `base_loader.py` was gated on `is_cuda()` only, so it never fired on ROCm. Changed to `is_cuda_alike()` which covers both CUDA and ROCm since `torch.accelerator.max_memory_allocated()` works on both.

2. The memory thresholds in `test_online_quant_peak_mem` were hardcoded for CUDA's `fp8_e4m3fn`. ROCm on gfx950 uses `fp8_e4m3fnuz` which results in slightly higher memory usage (~7.45 GiB vs ~6.65 GiB), so the thresholds are now platform-aware.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [CI][AMD][BugFix] Update request URL in test_moriio_connector to match vllm-router compatibility changes
prs.author: rasmith
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This [PR ](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/39565) changed the moriio KV connector to make it compatible with `vllm-router`, which changes the format of the request url to include the prefill, decode, and uuid values.  However, the tests in test_moriio_connector were not updated.

The requests generated did not include the changes to the URL needed to maintain compatibility.  This PR updates the test to generate requests that are compatible with the new pattern matching implemented to support `vllm-router` compatibility.
## Test Plan
`pytest -sv  v1/kv_connector/unit/test_moriio_connector.py`
## Test Result
`5 passed, 18 warnings in 5.13s`

prs.title: Enable building MoRI with AMD AINIC stack
prs.author: ichbinblau
prs.body_head:
## Summary


Updates the ROCm  image build (`docker/Dockerfile.rocm` and `docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base`) so MORI can be built with an optional **AMD AINIC (Pensando / ionic)** or **BNXT (broadcom)" NIC stack, following the same approach as SGLang’s [docker/rocm.Dockerfile](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/docker/rocm.Dockerfile#L366) MORI section.

Also bumps the default **MORI** git pin to `v1.1.0`, adds **MoriIO proxy** Python dependencies (`blinker`, `quart`, `aiohttp`, `msgpack`, `pyzmq`), and records NIC backend / AINIC version in `/app/versions.txt`.

Co-authored with: @billishyahao 

## Motivation

- Upgrade MoRI to `v1.1.0`
- Support **`NIC_BACKEND=ainic`** when building the MORI wheel so users targeting AMD Pensando NICs get **`libionic-dev`** / **`ionic-common`**.
- Install common **MoriIO proxy** Python packages in the dev base image for disaggregated / KV workflows that use the proxy.

## How to build

Default (no ionic):

```bash
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker buildx build \
            --file docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base \
            --tag rocm/vllm-dev:base \
            .
```

With AINIC / ionic:

``` bash
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \
          --build-arg max_jobs=16 \
          --build-arg NIC_BACKEND=ainic \
          --build-arg BASE_IMAGE=rocm/vllm-dev:base \
          --tag  rocm/vllm-dev:latest \
          -f docker/Dockerfile.rocm .
``` 
Optional: `--build-arg AINIC_VERSION=... `

## Test Plan
1. `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.rocm . ` (default NIC_BACKEND=none) completes.
2. `docker build --build-arg NIC_BACKEND=ainic -f docker/Dockerfile.rocm .` completes where AMD AINIC repo is reachable.
3. Inspect `/app/versions.txt` in the resulting image for `MORI_NIC_BACKEND/AINIC_VERSION` .

## Test Result

---

<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [ ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR wil

prs.title: [ROCm] Fuse Q/K RMSNorm in MLA attention via AITER `fused_qk_rmsnorm`
prs.author: xaguilar-amd
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Fuse the two separate RMSNorm kernel launches (`q_a_layernorm` and `kv_a_layernorm`) in Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) into a single AITER `fused_qk_rmsnorm` kernel on ROCm. This eliminates one GPU kernel launch per transformer layer per decode step for DeepSeek V2/V3/Kimi models.

## Motivation

During decode on MI3XX with DeepSeek V3 / Kimi-K2.5-MXFP4, the MLA attention block performs two consecutive RMSNorm operations on the compressed latent tensors:

1. `q_a_layernorm(q_c)` -- normalizes the compressed query latent (dim=512)
2. `kv_a_layernorm(kv_c)` -- normalizes the compressed key-value latent (dim=1536)

Each is a separate GPU kernel launch. At decode time, where per-kernel compute is ~4-5us but kernel launch overhead is significant, eliminating launches directly improves latency.

AITER provides a `fused_qk_rmsnorm` kernel that fuses both operations into a single launch using a 2D grid where `blockIdx.y` selects the Q (y=0) or K (y=1) normalization path, running both in parallel across different CUs.

**Impact**: Eliminates 1 kernel launch per layer per step. With 61 layers in DeepSeek V3, this saves 61 kernel launches per decode step.


## Design decisions

- **Model-level change, not a fusion pass**: The existing `QKNormRoPEFusionPass` targets a different pattern (standard QKV per-head norm + RoPE). The MLA pattern has different tensor shapes (latent-space norms on dims 512 and 1536, not per-head norms) and the two norms are separated from RoPE by a GEMM (`q_b_proj`). A direct model-level change in `mla.py` is more reliable than a graph pattern match.
- **Guarded behind AITER availability**: The fused path only activates when all conditions are met: ROCm platform, AITER installed, AITER rmsnorm enabled via env var. All other platforms and configurations use the existing two-norm path with zero behavioral change.
- **AITER's built-in fallback**: The AITER `fused_qk_rmsnorm` function automatically falls back to two separ
